title: Crisp Iced Tunisian Red Onions Tagine
description: A refreshing and flavorful breakfast dish from Tunisia, made with crisp iced red onions, ideal for curing hangovers. This recipe is low-glycemic-friendly and features a bain-marie cooking method.
instructions: Place a large metal bowl over a pot of simmering water to create a bain-marie.
In the bowl, combine the sliced red onions, olive oil, apple cider vinegar, water, coriander powder, salt, and black pepper.
Stir well to combine, then cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let it sit at room temperature for 30 minutes.
After 30 minutes, remove the bowl from the bain-marie and let it cool to room temperature.
Once cooled, stir in the chopped mint leaves and lemon juice.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Just before serving, place the iced onions on a bed of ice cubes and garnish with additional mint leaves if desired.
Serve the crisp iced onions with crusty bread on the side and enjoy your refreshing breakfast treat.
